insight: For Egypt, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a break, with a mean change of -0.050 minutes, indicating a slight decline in their performance. However, when they're not pressing before a break, they've managed to gain some momentum afterwards, with an average change of +0.100 minutes, suggesting that taking a step back and regrouping can actually help them come back stronger. In contrast, when they are pressing before a break, they've struggled to maintain their momentum, losing an average of 0.200 minutes, which could indicate that their high-intensity approach leaves them more vulnerable to a drop-off after a pause in play.
